title: Keep DAG structure deterministic and parse-light
description: Build dynamic DAGs from stable inputs and keep top-level parse code cheap so the scheduler stays fast and reproducible.
body: Use when generating tasks dynamically from a config or dataset. Do not query a live, changing source at DAG-parse time — the graph then changes shape unpredictably and the scheduler slows or breaks. Actions: generate dynamic tasks from a stable, versioned input, keep top-level parsing lightweight (no heavy I/O), and ensure the same input yields the same graph. Validation signals include a DAG whose task set is reproducible from its input and a parse time well within the scheduler budget. Risk boundary: heavy or nondeterministic parse code degrades the whole scheduler, not just one DAG; if parse time spikes, treat it as a platform risk and escalate to move work out of parse time.
